question:
After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.
Had it been possible to leave the country then, I would have done so.This means that
options: I have not left the country and nothing will make me leave
I wanted to leave the country and I did
I will definitely leave the country one day
I would have liked to leave the countyr but I wasn't able to

question:
After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.
The proprietor and his accountant have had a running battle over the last financial report. This means that they have had
options: an argument which developed into a fight
a series of arguments on the report
to work hard to complete the report

question:
After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.
Unless you don't want to become a teacher, there's no reason for not accepting the sponsorship. This means that you should
options: refuse the sponsorship if you want to become a teacher
not refuse the sponsorship if you don't want to become a teacher
refuse the sponsorship whether you want to become a teacher or not

question:
After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.
His chances of passing are very slim. This means that he:
explanation: If someone's chances are 'very slim,' it means they are highly unlikely to succeed.

question:
After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.
We would never have followed his advice if we had known his reputation. This means that we:
options: knew what people said about him so we refused to obey him
trusted him but did not need any advice
followed his advice because we didn't know him
trusted him but did not take his advice
explanation: The conditional statement implies the speakers followed the advice only because they were unaware of his reputation at the time.

question: The second sentence suggests that Tony's farm is good:
options: For escaping from the city noise
As a location for serious meetings

question: From the description of the farm and surrounding, we can infer that the barbed wire is
------ They receive their regular clinical attention, this means that
options: The animals are taken to the clinic regularly
The animals are under constant observation
The animals are given regular vaccination
The animals have a doctor at the farm

question: The farmers who use the animal droppings from Tony's farm think that:
options: Their farm will be a model

question: Tony's farm Manager can be described as

find under each sentence the group of words that give the nearest meaning to the idiom
He killed <u>the goose that laid the golden eggs</u> when he decided to spend his capital on buying a car
options: Sacrificed future profits to satisfy present needs

question:
find under each sentence the group of words that give the nearest meaning to the idiom.
I laughed up my sleeve as he told the tales of his exploits
options: Was openly making fun of him

question: Choose the word or set of words that best fits the meaning of the sentence as a whole: _________ the long run, we will make enough profit.
explanation: The correct prepositional phrase is 'In the long run.'

question: Choose the word or set of words that best fits the meaning of the sentence as a whole: The congregation _________ all sorts and conditions of men.
explanation: 'Comprises' is used to mean 'is made up of' or 'contains.'

question: Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word: The effect of the new policy on the masses is rather <u>negligible</u>.
explanation: Negligible means so small or unimportant as to be not worth considering; 'insignificant' is the closest synonym.
